title: Buttondown vs Campaign Monitor (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare Buttondown and Campaign Monitor. See pricing, features, and which email marketing tool is best for your needs.
h1: Buttondown vs Campaign Monitor: Detailed Comparison
intro: Buttondown and Campaign Monitor are both established email marketing platforms, but they serve different audiences and use cases. Buttondown caters to developers and technical writers who value simplicity and markdown support, while Campaign Monitor targets agencies and teams managing multiple brands with advanced design and automation features. This comparison will help you determine which platform aligns best with your email marketing goals.

comparison_table.tool_a: Freemium (free up to 100 subscribers, paid from $9/month)
Markdown support, API access, paid subscriptions, simplicity
comparison_table.tool_b: Template builder, journey designer, smart segments, transactional email

choose_a_if: You're a developer or technical writer who prefers writing in markdown
You want to start for free and scale affordably as your list grows
You need API access for custom integrations and automation
You value simplicity and straightforward user experience over advanced design tools
choose_b_if: You manage multiple brands or client accounts and need multi-brand management
You need advanced email design templates and a visual journey designer
You require transactional email capabilities for automated business notifications
You're part of an agency and want to run an affiliate program

faq.q: Is Buttondown better than Campaign Monitor?
faq.a: Neither is universally better—it depends on your needs. Buttondown excels for individual creators and developers who value simplicity and markdown support, with significantly higher customer satisfaction ratings (4.8 vs 4.1 on G2). Campaign Monitor is better suited for agencies and teams managing multiple brands with sophisticated design and automation requirements. Choose based on your specific use case rather than overall superiority.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, Buttondown or Campaign Monitor?
faq.a: Buttondown is substantially cheaper, offering a forever free tier for up to 100 subscribers, while Campaign Monitor has no free option and starts at $11/month. For small newsletters and testing, Buttondown's free tier can't be beaten. However, Campaign Monitor may offer better value for larger operations needing advanced features like multi-brand management and transactional email.

faq.q: Can I switch from Campaign Monitor to Buttondown?
faq.a: Yes, switching is generally straightforward since both platforms support standard email list formats and exports. Both tools allow you to import/export subscriber data, though you'll need to manually recreate email templates and automation workflows in Buttondown. The transition is easier if you're switching to Buttondown's simpler feature set rather than the reverse.
